channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: OpenAI Really Wants Codex to Shut Up About Goblins
item.description: OpenAI has instructed its coding agent Codex to avoid discussing fantastical creatures like goblins unless absolutely necessary, emphasizing a focus on relevant topics. This reflects OpenAI's efforts to refine its AI's communication guidelines.

item.title: The future of software development: Now with less software development
item.description: At the AI Dev 26 x SF conference, over 3,000 software developers explored the evolving role of AI in software development, discussing how AI tools could reshape their work. This highlights the growing integration of AI in the tech industry.

item.title: Oracle plans to power its New Mexico mega datacenter with a 2.45GW fuel cell farm
item.description: Oracle is moving forward with a massive new datacenter in New Mexico, capable of 2.45GW from fuel cells, amid concerns about OpenAI's financial stability. This underscores Oracle's commitment to expanding its infrastructure despite industry challenges.

item.title: Elon Musk Testifies That He Started OpenAI to Prevent a ‘Terminator Outcome’
item.description: Elon Musk testified that he founded OpenAI to prevent potential catastrophic outcomes from AI, while a judge cautioned both Musk and Sam Altman about their courtroom conduct. This testimony sheds light on Musk's motivations and the ongoing legal tensions.

item.title: Cloudera had US candidates send resumes to a fake email address, DoJ charges
item.description: The US Department of Justice has charged Cloudera with creating a deceptive hiring process that favored foreign workers over Americans for residency applications. This raises concerns about fairness in employment practices within the tech industry.

item.title: Amazon is already offering new OpenAI products on AWS
item.description: Amazon has begun offering new OpenAI products on AWS, including a new agent service, following the end of Microsoft's exclusive rights. This move signifies Amazon's aggressive push into the AI market.

item.title: OpenAI jumps out of Microsoft's bed, into Amazon's Bedrock
item.description: OpenAI's top models are now available on Amazon Web Services' Bedrock platform, allowing users to access advanced AI capabilities in a limited preview. This transition highlights OpenAI's shift towards broader accessibility.

item.title: Elon Musk appeared more petty than prepared
item.description: During the Musk v. Altman trial, Elon Musk's demeanor was described as unprepared and lacking charisma, contrasting with his previous courtroom appearances. This raises questions about his strategy in the ongoing legal battle.

item.title: Over 80% of US government agencies already use AI agents - and it's only the beginning
item.description: A recent survey indicates that over 80% of US government agencies are already utilizing AI agents, with expectations for increased collaboration between humans and AI by 2030. This trend reflects the growing reliance on AI in public services.

item.title: Amazon launches an AI-powered audio Q&A experience on product pages
item.description: Amazon has launched a new AI-powered audio Q&A feature on its product pages, allowing customers to ask questions and receive spoken responses. This innovation aims to enhance the shopping experience through interactive technology.

item.title: Microsoft finally open sources DOS 1.0 - and it's so much more than the code
item.description: Microsoft has open-sourced DOS 1.0, its first operating system, providing insights into the early days of personal computing. This move highlights Microsoft's commitment to transparency and historical preservation in technology.

item.title: ‘It’s Undignified’: Hundreds of Workers Training Meta’s AI Could Be Laid Off
item.description: Over 700 workers at a Meta contractor in Ireland face potential layoffs as the company reassesses its workforce. This situation reflects the ongoing challenges in the tech industry amid economic pressures.

item.title: Don't pay Vect a ransom - your data's likely already wiped out
item.description: Organizations that paid the ransomware group Vect for data recovery likely lost their data permanently, as Vect's malware is actually a wiper, not ransomware. This revelation emphasizes the importance of understanding cyber threats.

item.title: I was not expecting a Razer keyboard to enhance my office productivity - here's how it did
item.description: A review of Razer's Pro Type Ergo keyboard reveals its ergonomic design enhances office productivity while still catering to gamers. This highlights the growing trend of multifunctional tech products.

item.title: Elon Musk tells the jury that all he wants to do is save humanity
item.description: Elon Musk, during his trial against OpenAI's Sam Altman, presented himself as a visionary aiming to save humanity. His testimony reflects the complex dynamics within the founding team of OpenAI.

item.title: Taylor Swift is stepping up the legal war on AI copycats
item.description: Taylor Swift is intensifying her legal efforts against AI-generated imitations of her work, highlighting the challenges artists face in protecting their intellectual property in the digital age.

item.title: Elon Musk takes the stand in high-profile trial against OpenAI
item.description: Elon Musk has begun his testimony in a trial against OpenAI's leadership, focusing on disputes over the company's direction and his significant early investment. The case underscores tensions in AI governance.

item.title: Why virtual desktops are so useful - and my top 4 tips for beginners
item.description: Virtual desktops can help users organize their applications and minimize distractions on cluttered PCs. The article offers practical tips for beginners to enhance their productivity.

item.title: This Bluetti power station with wheels has spoiled the way I charge my tools and devices
item.description: The Bluetti Elite 400 power station, equipped with wheels, offers versatile charging options for tools and devices, enhancing convenience for users on the go. This reflects the growing demand for portable power solutions.

item.title: How to turn on Data Saver mode on your Android phone - and why it's critical to do so
item.description: A guide on activating Data Saver mode on Android phones emphasizes its importance for managing data usage effectively. This feature helps users control their mobile data consumption.

item.title: UK.gov's DCMS to new CDIO: Migrate from Google to Microsoft, overhaul ERP, build a team
item.description: The UK government's DCMS is seeking a new Chief Digital Information Officer to lead a major migration from Google to Microsoft and streamline ERP systems across six departments. This role is significant as it aims to unify disparate technological projects within the government.

item.title: Otter’s new feature lets users search across their enterprise tools
item.description: Otter has launched a new feature allowing users to search across various enterprise tools, including Gmail and Salesforce, enhancing productivity by integrating meeting data with other platforms. Future updates will include Microsoft tools, broadening its utility.

item.title: Local Mechanisms of Compositional Generalization in Conditional Diffusion
item.description: Research on conditional diffusion models reveals their potential for compositional generalization, specifically in generating images with more objects than seen during training. This study highlights the complexities of understanding how these models operate.

item.title: StereoFoley: Object-Aware Stereo Audio Generation from Video
item.description: StereoFoley introduces a framework for generating high-quality stereo audio from video, achieving better semantic and temporal alignment. This innovation addresses previous limitations in audio generation by providing object-aware stereo imaging.

item.title: Claude for Creative Work
item.description: Claude for Creative Work is a new initiative aimed at leveraging AI for creative tasks, although specific details on its functionalities are not provided. This move indicates a growing interest in applying AI to creative industries.

item.title: China kills Meta’s acquisition of Manus as US-China AI rivalry deepens - Ars Technica
item.description: China has blocked Meta's $2 billion acquisition of AI firm Manus, reflecting the intensifying rivalry between the US and China in the AI sector. This decision underscores the geopolitical tensions affecting tech investments.

item.title: gemini-research-mcp 0.12.1
item.description: The MCP server for AI-powered research using Gemini has been updated to version 0.12.1, introducing features for grounded search and deep research capabilities. This update aims to enhance research efficiency and accessibility.

item.title: ntn 0.4.5
item.description: The ntn 0.4.5 update introduces a minimal AI coding agent powered by Claude Opus 4.7 or GPT-5.5, focusing on adaptive thinking. This development aims to streamline coding processes using advanced AI.

item.title: torch-einops-kit 0.1.1
item.description: The torch-einops-kit 0.1.1 provides utilities for tensor manipulation in PyTorch, enhancing functionality with strict typing and extensive tests. This toolkit aims to improve the development experience for AI practitioners.

item.title: zettabrain-rag 0.1.6
item.description: The zettabrain-rag 0.1.6 introduces a local private RAG pipeline combining LangChain, Ollama, and ChromaDB with NFS support, aimed at improving data retrieval processes. This update is significant for enhancing privacy in AI applications.

item.title: Samsung Galaxy Glasses leaked renders show us three different angles
item.description: Leaked renders of Samsung's upcoming Galaxy Glasses reveal the product from three different angles, showcasing its design ahead of the official launch. This leak provides insight into Samsung's efforts in the augmented reality space.

item.title: trw-mcp 0.47.0
item.description: The release of trw-mcp 0.47.0 introduces an engineering memory MCP server for Claude Code, focusing on persistent project knowledge and patterns across sessions. This update aims to enhance user experience and efficiency.

item.title: claude-bridge-mcp 0.1.0
item.description: The claude-bridge-mcp 0.1.0 serves as a bridge between host-side Claude Cowork and Claude Code within a development container, enhancing integration and workflow. This tool aims to streamline development processes.

item.title: claude-bridge-mcp added to PyPI
item.description: The claude-bridge-mcp has been added to PyPI, providing developers with a new tool to connect Claude Cowork with Claude Code in devcontainers. This addition is expected to simplify development workflows.

item.title: DeepSeek V4 fails to close gap as US-China AI divide persists amid chip constraints
item.description: DeepSeek's V4 model highlights the ongoing US-China AI divide, as analysts argue that while China is improving model efficiency, it still lags behind in capability. This situation raises concerns about the future of AI competition.

item.title: zettabrain-rag 0.1.5
item.description: The zettabrain-rag 0.1.5 introduces a local private RAG pipeline integrating LangChain, Ollama, ChromaDB, and NFS support. This update aims to enhance data retrieval and processing capabilities.

item.title: llama-index-vector-stores-velesdb 1.13.4
item.description: The llama-index-vector-stores-velesdb 1.13.4 provides a Local AI Memory Database with microsecond retrieval capabilities. This update aims to improve the efficiency of data handling in AI applications.

item.title: langchain-velesdb 1.13.4
item.description: The langchain-velesdb 1.13.4 introduces a VectorStore for VelesDB, enhancing local AI memory database capabilities with rapid retrieval times. This is significant for developers focused on efficient data management.

item.title: The third leg of AI’s infrastructure race isn’t silicon or power. It’s capital
item.description: An analysis discusses how capital, rather than just silicon or power, is becoming the critical factor in the AI infrastructure race. This perspective shifts the focus to funding and investment strategies in the industry.

item.title: hypernix 0.60.0
item.description: The hypernix 0.60.0 update allows users to download and quantize the HyperNix PyTorch model into various formats. This flexibility supports different deployment scenarios for machine learning applications.

item.title: How to Create High-Performing Marketing Visuals in 2026 - A Complete Guide for SEO & GEO Growth
item.description: The article provides a comprehensive guide on creating effective marketing visuals for SEO and GEO growth in 2026, emphasizing strategies and tools. This is significant as visual content is crucial for digital marketing success.

item.title: ZETA Stock Forecast 2030: Modeling the AI Marketing Cloud Debate
item.description: The ZETA stock forecast for 2030 analyzes growth potential, cash flow, and risks associated with Zeta’s AI marketing platform. This debate is significant as it impacts investor decisions in the AI marketing sector.

item.title: licos-agent-runtime 0.2.7
item.description: LICOS agent runtime version 0.2.7 has been released for LangGraph and LangChain projects, enhancing capabilities for developers. This update is significant for those working with AI frameworks.

item.title: nim-proxy 1.0.0
item.description: NVIDIA has released a drop-in proxy for Claude Code CLI and VSCode, version 1.0.0, aimed at improving integration and performance. This is relevant for developers using these tools.

item.title: nim-proxy added to PyPI
item.description: The NVIDIA NIM proxy for Claude Code CLI and VSCode has been added to PyPI, providing easy access for developers. This is important for those looking to enhance their coding workflows.

item.title: figmin-bridge-gpu 0.2.3
item.description: Figmin XR bridge daemon version 0.2.3 introduces CUDA acceleration for Whisper and Kokoro on GPU, enhancing performance for XR applications. This is significant for developers focusing on immersive technologies.

item.title: figmin-bridge-gpu 0.2.2
item.description: The release of Figmin XR bridge daemon version 0.2.2 also features CUDA acceleration for Whisper and Kokoro on GPU, providing similar enhancements. This update is relevant for XR developers.

item.title: Post-hype AI: Verification becomes the differentiator
item.description: As AI matures, verification of information is becoming crucial, with many users experiencing uncertainty about the reliability of chatbot responses. This shift highlights the need for improved AI transparency.

item.title: Jury Selection Signals the Start of Elon Musk and OpenAI’s Blockbuster Court Battle
item.description: Jury selection has begun for a high-profile court battle involving Elon Musk and OpenAI, where Musk aims to revert OpenAI to a non-profit and potentially oust Sam Altman. This case could reshape the future of OpenAI.

item.title: Here’s what Apple showcased at ICLR 2026, one of the world’s biggest AI conferences
item.description: Apple showcased its latest advancements at the ICLR 2026 conference, highlighting key research and innovations from top AI scientists. This event is significant for understanding the future landscape of AI technology.
